# Changelog

## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: This PR adds a guard in the `load_config` function to raise a clean `FileNotFoundError` when the YAML configuration file is missing or empty. Previously, attempting to load a missing config would result in an unhelpful error when trying to access dictionary keys on a `None` value. Now, the function explicitly checks if `config_dict` is `None` after reading the YAML file and raises a descriptive error message that clearly indicates the config file was not found or is empty.
This needs to handle the case where `tokens` is empty — currently it raises IndexError.
A few issues with edge cases — see inline. Otherwise looks great.

# Changelog

## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client
- Fix incorrect cost-per-token for Haiku 4.5
- Drop Python 3.11 support

## 0.3.0 (2026-04-22)

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: Previously, `load_config` would raise an unhelpful `TypeError` (or silently misbehave) when the target YAML file was missing or empty, because `read_yaml` would return `None` and the subsequent dictionary access would fail without a clear message. This PR adds an explicit guard immediately after the `read_yaml` call: if `config_dict is None`, a descriptive `FileNotFoundError` is raised with the offending path included in the message. This makes misconfiguration failures immediately obvious to callers and easier to handle upstream, replacing a cryptic traceback with a clean, actionable error.
input.head: fix/null-config-handling

input.body: This needs to handle the case where `tokens` is empty — currently it raises IndexError.
A few issues with edge cases — see inline. Otherwise looks great.

input.message: fix: point clients to prod URL

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87 — _"DB connection pool: cursor.execute() interleaves under concurrent load"_.

### Problem
Under concurrent load, multiple threads were sharing the same `psycopg2` connection and calling `cursor.execute()` simultaneously, causing interleaved queries and corrupted results.

### Fix
Introduced a `threading.Lock` (`self._lock`) in `src/db.py` and wrapped the `cursor.execute()` call in a `with self._lock:` block, serializing all database access through a single connection and eliminating the race condition.

### Changed Files
- `src/db.py` — added `threading.Lock` and serialized cursor access

### Testing
Verify under concurrent load that queries no longer interleave. Regression tests for the connection pool should be run as part of CI.
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race

## Summary

This PR introduces a dark mode theme to the application across three commits:

- **feat(themes): add dark mode stylesheet** — adds `src/themes/dark.css` with CSS custom properties (`--bg`, `--fg`, `--accent`) scoped to `:root[data-theme='dark']`.
- **feat(themes): wire dark mode into theme loader** — appends `@import './dark.css';` to `src/themes/index.css` so the new stylesheet is picked up by the theme loader.
- **test(themes): add dark mode test stub** — adds `tests/test_dark_mode.py` with a placeholder test (`test_dark_mode_loads`) to be wired up to the theme loader in a follow-up.

## Files Changed

- `src/themes/dark.css` *(new)*
- `src/themes/index.css` *(modified)*
- `tests/test_dark_mode.py` *(new)*

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race
input.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87 — _"DB connection pool: cursor.execute() interleaves under concurrent load"_.

Under concurrent load, multiple threads were calling `cursor.execute()` on the same connection simultaneously, causing interleaved queries and corrupted result sets.

## Change

**`src/db.py`** — wraps the cursor creation and `cursor.execute()` call inside a `threading.Lock` (`self._lock`), serializing all database access through a single connection and eliminating the race condition.

```python
def execute(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> list:
    with self._lock:          # ← added
        cursor = self._conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute(sql, params)
        return cursor.fetchall()
```

## Testing

- Reproduce the race with a concurrent-load harness against the unpatched code → observe interleaved results.
- Apply patch → same harness produces consistent, correct results across all threads.

## Risk

Low — change is strictly additive (adds a lock); no existing call-sites are modified. The lock does serialize queries, so throughput under high concurrency will be limited; a connection-pool approach can be a follow-up if needed.

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87 — under concurrent load, `cursor.execute()` calls in the connection pool were interleaving, causing data corruption and unexpected errors.

## Changes

- **`src/db.py`**: Introduced a `threading.Lock` (`self._lock`) on the `DB` class and wrapped the `cursor.execute()` call in a `with self._lock:` block to serialize all cursor access, eliminating the race condition.

## Root Cause

The `DB.execute()` method was not thread-safe. Multiple threads sharing the same `DB` instance could simultaneously enter `cursor.execute()`, leading to interleaved queries on the same connection.

## Fix

```python
def execute(self, sql: str, params: tuple = ()) -> list:
    with self._lock:
        cursor = self._conn.cursor()
        cursor.execute(sql, params)
        return cursor.fetchall()
```

## Testing

- Reproducer from #87 (concurrent load against the connection pool) should no longer exhibit interleaving.
- Existing unit tests for `src/db.py` continue to pass.
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race

input.title: Add dark mode theme
input.body:
## Summary

This PR introduces a dark mode theme to the application across three commits:

1. **feat(themes): add dark mode stylesheet** — Adds `src/themes/dark.css` with CSS custom properties for the dark theme (`--bg`, `--fg`, `--accent`) scoped to `:root[data-theme='dark']`.

2. **feat(themes): wire dark mode into theme loader** — Appends `@import './dark.css';` to `src/themes/index.css` so the new dark stylesheet is automatically loaded by the theme system.

3. **test(themes): add dark mode test stub** — Adds `tests/test_dark_mode.py` with a placeholder test (`test_dark_mode_loads`) to be wired up to the theme loader in a follow-up.

## Files Changed
- `src/themes/dark.css` *(new)*
- `src/themes/index.css` *(modified)*
- `tests/test_dark_mode.py` *(new)*
input.head: feature/dark-mode

## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: Previously, `load_config` would raise an unhelpful `TypeError` (or silently misbehave) when the target YAML file was missing or empty, because `read_yaml` would return `None` and the subsequent dictionary access would fail without a clear message. This PR adds an explicit guard immediately after the `read_yaml` call: if `config_dict is None`, a descriptive `FileNotFoundError` is raised with the offending path included in the message. This makes misconfiguration errors immediately obvious to callers and easier to handle upstream.
input.head: fix/null-config-handling

input.body: This needs to handle the case where `tokens` is empty — currently it raises IndexError.
A few issues with edge cases — see inline. Otherwise looks great.

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87 — `cursor.execute()` was interleaving under concurrent load due to unsynchronized access to the shared DB connection.

### Root Cause

`src/db.py`'s `DB.execute()` method was not thread-safe: multiple threads could call `cursor.execute()` concurrently on the same connection, causing interleaved queries and corrupted results.

### Fix

Added a `threading.Lock` (`self._lock`) to `src/db.py` and wrapped the `cursor.execute()` call in a `with self._lock:` block, serializing all DB access and eliminating the race condition.

### Files Changed

- `src/db.py` — added `threading.Lock` and guarded `cursor.execute()` with `with self._lock:`

### Testing

Verified under simulated concurrent load that queries no longer interleave. Reproducer from #87 passes cleanly after this patch.
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: Previously, `load_config` would raise an unhelpful `TypeError` (or silently misbehave) when the target YAML file was missing or empty, because `read_yaml` would return `None` and the subsequent dictionary access would fail without a clear message. This PR adds an explicit guard immediately after the `read_yaml` call in `load_config`: if `config_dict is None`, a descriptive `FileNotFoundError` is raised with the offending path included in the message. This makes misconfiguration failures immediately obvious to callers and easier to handle upstream, replacing a cryptic traceback with a clean, actionable error.
input.head: fix/null-config-handling

input.title: Hotfix: DB cursor race condition
input.head: hotfix/db-cursor-race
input.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87 — _"DB connection pool: cursor.execute() interleaves under concurrent load"_.

### Root Cause
`cursor.execute()` in `src/db.py` was not protected by any synchronization primitive, allowing concurrent threads to interleave calls on the same connection and corrupt query results.

### Fix
Introduced a `threading.Lock` (`self._lock`) on the `DB` class and wrapped the cursor creation and execution block in `src/db.py` with `with self._lock:`, serializing all database access through a single connection.

### Changed Files
- `src/db.py` — added `threading.Lock` and `with self._lock:` guard around `cursor.execute()`

### Testing
- Reproducer from #87 (concurrent load against the connection pool) no longer produces interleaved results after this patch.
- Existing unit tests pass.

- **feat(themes): wire dark mode into theme loader** — appends `@import './dark.css';` to `src/themes/index.css` so the dark stylesheet is picked up by the theme loader automatically.
